title: ActiveCampaign vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign vs Sendinblue. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool wins for your business needs.
intro: ActiveCampaign and Sendinblue (now Brevo) are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign excels in advanced automation and CRM capabilities, while Sendinblue offers a generous free tier and transactional email focus. This comparison will help you decide which platform aligns best with your marketing strategy and budget.

comparison_table.tool_a: Advanced automation workflows and dynamic content
comparison_table.tool_b: Basic automation; stronger in transactional email

choose_a_if: You need advanced marketing automation and sophisticated lead scoring
Building complex customer journeys is a priority for your business
You want tight CRM integration with email marketing capabilities
choose_b_if: You're starting out and need a free or low-cost option
You primarily send transactional emails and SMS campaigns
You want omnichannel communication including WhatsApp and chat

faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign is superior for advanced automation, lead scoring, and CRM-heavy workflows, making it ideal for mid-market and enterprise businesses. Sendinblue is better for cost-conscious startups and teams focused on transactional email and SMS. Both have strong ratings (4.5/5 on G2), so your choice should depend on your specific use case.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Sendinblue is significantly cheaper for most businesses. It offers a free tier with 300 emails per day and up to 100,000 subscribers, while ActiveCampaign has no free option and starts at $15/month. However, if you need advanced automation features, ActiveCampaign's pricing becomes more competitive relative to its capabilities.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support data export and migration. Most email marketing tools allow you to export your contact lists, though you may need to manually recreate automation workflows. ActiveCampaign offers migration support for enterprise customers, so contact their team for assistance with a smooth transition.
